effects.reason: ask Lev about the earlier signal
ask Arkady to name the event
hold the useful act beside the claim
ask about the people in the photograph
ask where Gennady stands in the photograph
leave the answer where it is
ask Lev for the missed exchange
watch the room after the report
let the room absorb the repetition

entries.text: The television clears. Arkady lowers his voice. Galina signals from the counter.
Watch the tables. The room is ahead of its glasses again.
One glass leans near a photograph. Arkady has squared his papers. Lev is listening to the set.
A thin instrumental phrase sits under the receiver hiss. The television picture rolls once.
The picture moves across Lev's glasses. He listens before he looks.
Tea. Good. Leave the bottle to argue with itself.
Better. Do not mistake better for true.
The phrase ends. The receiver keeps breathing into the room.
Bosses change chairs. People who make things remain.
I trained people who stopped needing my hand on the work.
Galina has kept this room working. Does that count?
You count the work after it is done. I count it before you notice.
Work does not cancel responsibility. Neither does an order.
An emergency can require measures no one would choose in ordinary conditions.
Authority is not a chair occupied by one person. It is a chain of duties.
Once, the papers meant heat reached homes before the cold did.
Necessity is not approval. Those are separate terms.
A clean signal can still carry a bad guess.
The picture thins. Nikolai's tea waits. Gennady's glass shifts against its wet ring.
There. It may hold until someone needs certainty from it.
Nikolai takes the tea with both hands and leaves the glass alone.
The sound changed before the picture. The set is receiving something new.
The last note fades. A chair leg answers from the far table.
That glass goes now, or it goes by itself.
You clear the glass. A factory photograph lies beneath its wet ring.
The glass tips. Galina catches the spill while Gennady saves the photograph.
They knew the work. Most of them learned it from me.
The good ones argued with me. Then they became better than me.
A photograph keeps the shift tidy. The shift was not tidy.
Reports belonged to the office. We kept the shop moving.
I helped hide what happened. I still visit the man who paid for it.
You return the photograph. Gennady covers it with his hand.
The signal clears long enough to hear an uncertain bulletin in full.
I opposed this from the beginning. There was never any question.
He changes his account when the room changes. Listen for the order.
The music is gone. The television stays on. For a moment, nobody helps the room move again.
The receiver hum returns beneath the chairs. Arkady still has to answer.
You said an emergency could require it. Now you say you opposed it from the start.
I heard you say something about opposing it from the start. What changed?
While you were away, Arkady discovered he had always objected.
Lev says your account changed. Did it?
The circumstances changed. Precision should change with them.
Gennady's photograph remains on the table.
Tired? I am working. Ask me again when that door is locked.
Worse. Earlier they were pretending the rolling picture was deliberate.
The man who carried out the order. He does not vanish with the paperwork.
A serious constitutional irregularity. Sasha, shouting a shorter word will not improve it.
It gives my hands something harmless to do.
Second from the left. The idiot telling everyone not to lean on the machine.
I believed he meant what he said at the time. That is not the same thing.
I wanted one person to know. I did not ask for a meeting.
That was said to you, not to this table.
Galina closes her notebook. She does not ask what was said.
The photograph is face-down. The room is still speaking.
The photograph stays face-up. Some parts of the room remain private.
Watch the picture move across Lev's glasses.
Was the signal this bad earlier?
What remains when the orders change?
And what does responsibility mean to you?
Name one thing the system got done.
Watch Lev listen to the change.
Why did you move the photograph?
Watch his hand on the photograph.
What was your part in it?
Leave that answer where it is.
Ask Lev for the missing words.
Ask with Lev named as the source.
Tell the table what Gennady admitted.
What do you want me to do with it?
Leave the room to its noise.
Gennady turned the factory photograph face-down after his private account was repeated.
You heard Gennady's account and did not give it to the room.
Whatever Gennady withheld remains outside your record.
You heard both versions of Arkady's position directly.
You caught only part of Arkady's later version.
Lev told you that Arkady had changed his account.
